title: Marketplace payment reconciliation (Recko pattern)
persona: Finance team at marketplace / commerce platform (Meesho/PharmEasy/Deliveroo-scale)
pain_description: Reconcile orders ↔ payment gateway ↔ bank ↔ refunds ↔ chargebacks ↔ FX. Millions of txns/month; mismatches buried in CSV.
incumbent_gap: Stripe owns Recko; adjacent categories (billing, subscriptions, payouts) still fragmented
santifer_pattern: Multi-source connectors + LLM matcher + exception queue + audit trail

verbatim_quotes.quote: On the app, the bill total is shown as INR 1,600 with no mention of a packaging charge. However, the total cost of the items only comes to INR 1,574.

reference_build: Recko. 50-60% manpower reduction; acquired by Stripe 2021
why_now: LLMs normalize messy CSVs; API connectors mature

specific_prospects.name: Meesho
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Active marketplace. Public investor updates. Finance team manages complex multi-currency, multi-payment-processor reconciliation.

specific_prospects.name: PharmEasy
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Pharmacy marketplace. Daily reconciliation across orders, payment gateways, bank transfers, refunds.

specific_prospects.name: r/fintech
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Regular discussion of reconciliation headaches. Founders of smaller marketplaces lurk here.

specific_prospects.name: Stripe (Recko acquirer)
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Stripe owns Recko. Potential acquisition target or partnership for adjacent reconciliation features.

specific_prospects.name: Ledge
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Direct competitor. Potential acquirer or partnership for SMB segment.

velocity_signal.evidence: Stripe acquired Recko to add reconciliation to payment stack (2021). Ledge raised Series B and expanded to marketplace segment (2024 to 2025). Multi-currency, multi-gateway reconciliation volume increasing as India cross-border commerce grows. RBI regulations (2024) require tighter accounting controls for payment platforms.
velocity_signal.trigger: Marketplace volume growth, multi-currency expansion, and regulatory tightening (RBI 2024 circular on fintech compliance). Every unicorn-scale marketplace now faces daily reconciliation burden.

buying_intent_evidence.quote: Reconcile orders to payment gateway to bank to refunds to chargebacks to foreign exchange. Millions of transactions per month. Mismatches buried in CSV.
Adjacent categories (billing, subscriptions, payouts) still fragmented. Stripe owns Recko but does not cover the full stack.

kill_risks.risk: Stripe incumbency: Stripe already owns Recko. Could add reconciliation natively to payment processing.
kill_risks.current_status: Stripe moving slowly on Recko integration. Mid-market marketplaces not yet on Stripe (many use Razorpay, PhonePe, custom integrations in India). Opportunity window exists for next 18 to 24 months.

kill_risks.risk: Data privacy and regulatory: finance teams hesitant to send reconciliation data to new SaaS vendor.
kill_risks.current_status: Standard financial institution controls (NDA, data residency, audit logs) address this. Ledge already proven model.

kill_risks.risk: Complex connector ecosystem: each marketplace has custom payment processor integrations, different bank APIs.
kill_risks.current_status: High engineering lift to support 5 to 10 major payment gateways in India. Requires dedicated integrations.

fermi_math_to_50k.notes: India-based marketplaces at Meesho / PharmEasy scale (and 1K to 2K mid-sized ones below) number approximately 1,200. Stripe / Ledge incumbency means low conversion (5 percent). Anchor price $5,000 per month. 10 customers at $5,000 per month = $50,000. At 1 to 2 new marketplace customers per month (conservative), reaches $50K in 12 months.

title: Card-fee & interchange overcharge recovery
pain_description: Processors overcharge interchange quietly; businesses lose 0.3-1.5% of GMV; reviewing fee schedules is a full-time job.
santifer_pattern: Txn feed + interchange rules engine + overcharge flagger + chargeback file generator

title: Bank reconciliation for multi-entity / multi-currency
pain_description: Intercompany transfers + FX + shared processor accounts reconciled in Excel; month-end delayed.
incumbent_gap: Workflow tools; not LLM matchers on narrative fields
santifer_pattern: Multi-bank ingest + LLM matcher on memo fields + intercompany elim agent

title: Subscription revenue recognition reconciliation
pain_description: CRM contracts + billing + payments + CPQ don't match; ASC 606 compliance manual; auditors flag.
incumbent_gap: Subscription billing tools; narrative RevRec weak
santifer_pattern: Contract RAG + billing events + ASC 606 agent + auditor memo

title: Chargeback dispute evidence packaging
pain_description: Chargeback comes; team pulls order + tracking + customer comms; packages evidence per processor template; deadlines tight.
santifer_pattern: Dispute intake + evidence RAG + processor-template formatter + submission agent

title: Spreadsheet replacement for ops teams (Smartsheet-style)
pain_description: Team runs ops in 20+ Excel files; version chaos; approvals over email; reports hand-built.
santifer_pattern: Airtable base + automation + LLM-generated views + approval agents

title: Construction PMO tracking across subcontractors
pain_description: Schedule + RFIs + submittals + daily logs + punch list across 15 subs; Smartsheet/Procore incomplete.
santifer_pattern: Project SSOT + WhatsApp/email intake + auto-RFI/submittal routing + dashboard

title: Approval workflow automation (generic r/automation)
pain_description: Approvals in email chains; PTO, expense, doc, vendor. stall, lost, re-asked.
incumbent_gap: Rule-based; no LLM reasoning on edge cases
santifer_pattern: Request intake + policy RAG + LLM approve-in-policy + escalate outliers

title: Data-entry automation: forms → CRM/ERP
pain_description: Web forms, emails, PDFs flow into CRM via copy-paste; hours lost; errors propagate.
santifer_pattern: Any-source intake + LLM field extraction + CRM/ERP push + audit

title: Multi-location home services: ServiceTitan alternative for mid-size
pain_description: ServiceTitan $400+/seat with implementation fees; small chains priced out; disparate tools.
incumbent_gap: Price gap between Jobber ($39) and ServiceTitan ($400+); mid-size underserved
santifer_pattern: SSOT + AI dispatch + per-job P&L + customer comms agent at SMB price

verbatim_quotes.quote: ServiceTitan costs $245-$500/tech/month, with $5,000-$15,000 setup fees, and 6-12 month onboarding.

specific_prospects.name: HVAC Contractors Association (mid-size chapters)
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Chapters sponsor regional meetups. Sponsor content on dispatch and per-job profit and loss.

specific_prospects.name: Jobber user community (budget-conscious contractors)
specific_prospects.signal_size: 10,000+ small contractors using Jobber at $39 per tech per month
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Reach out as an upgrade for contractors outgrowing Jobber but priced out of ServiceTitan.

specific_prospects.name: ServiceTitan defector threads
specific_prospects.why_reachable: ServiceTitan pricing at $400-plus per seat drives high frustration. Post an alternative.

specific_prospects.name: Plumbing contractors (NAPHCC)
specific_prospects.why_reachable: National Association of Plumbing Heating Cooling Contractors. Similar market.

specific_prospects.name: Franchise support networks (Aire Serv, Mr. Rooter)
specific_prospects.why_reachable: Franchise support teams actively seek cost-effective, multi-location tools.

velocity_signal.evidence: Field service management market growing 12 percent CAGR per Gartner 2024. ServiceTitan pricing ($400 to $500 per seat) is out of reach for 30 to 200 tech contractors. Jobber at $39 per tech per month captures the low end but lacks enterprise features. Home services labor spend is $39.4 billion nationally.
velocity_signal.trigger: Mid-market contractors (30 to 200 techs) outgrow Jobber but will not pay ServiceTitan pricing. Large pricing gap ($39 to $400-plus) creates a beachhead. Supply chain and labor volatility drive need for per-job economics visibility.

buying_intent_evidence.quote: ServiceTitan $400-plus per seat with implementation fees. Small chains priced out. Disparate tools.
ServiceTitan costs $245 to $500 per tech per month, with $5,000 to $15,000 setup fees, 6 to 12 month onboarding.

kill_risks.risk: Jobber ships enterprise features and raises pricing to $150 to $200 per tech per month.
kill_risks.current_status: Jobber raising capital. Premium tier likely by 2026.

kill_risks.risk: ServiceTitan or Housecall Pro launches a mid-market tier at $150 to $200 per tech per month, squeezing the gap.
kill_risks.current_status: Likely defensive move by incumbents. Risk rising as the market consolidates.

kill_risks.risk: Field service automation consolidation (Syncro acquiring category) bundles away the gap.
kill_risks.current_status: Real but the market remains fragmented. Multi-vertical plays (HVAC plus plumbing plus electrical) are still underserved by a single vendor.

fermi_math_to_50k.notes: About 1,500 to 2,500 mid-size HVAC and plumbing chains (30 to 200 techs) with dispatch and ops budget. Anchor $200 per month (blended between Jobber and ServiceTitan). 250 customers at $200 per month = $50,000 per month. Path: 10 pilots by month 8, 50 by month 16, 250 by month 32. Challenge: 3 percent conversion plus long sales cycle plus high customer acquisition cost. Needs 250 customers to hit $50,000 per month. Unit economics are tight: lifetime value of $200 times 24 months = $4,800. Needs customer acquisition cost under $1,500.

title: Trade shop intelligent dispatch: skill + zone + shift
pain_description: Dispatcher juggles skill fit, zone, shift, equipment, priority. by brain. Missed appointments, tech drive time waste.
santifer_pattern: Calendar + skill/zone/shift matrix + LLM recommender + auto-reassign on cancel

title: Field-tech upsell guidance: in-home estimating
pain_description: Tech either upsells too little or pushes unethically; scripts static; recurring customers churn.
santifer_pattern: Photo/voice from site + LLM options generator + good-better-best pricing + ethics guardrails

title: Trade shop CSR (customer service rep) coaching
pain_description: CSR books <50% of inbound; call recordings reviewed manually; training spotty.
santifer_pattern: Call transcript + booking rate + per-CSR coaching + script suggestions

title: Task automation across 20 tools (Zapier/Make frustration)
persona: Ops lead drowning in SaaS sprawl
pain_description: Integration debt: 20 tools, 200 Zaps, breaks constantly; debugging painful; maintainer is a cost center.
santifer_pattern: Natural-language workflow + self-healing agent + drift-detector

title: Shared mailbox triage: sales@, support@, info@
pain_description: Shared mailboxes drown; team member copies to CRM/ticket; coverage spotty; leads lost.
santifer_pattern: Inbox intake + classifier + auto-reply + CRM/ticket push + human handoff

title: SOP/playbook adherence in ops
pain_description: Onboarding, offboarding, customer kickoff SOPs skipped under pressure; quality erodes.
santifer_pattern: SOP SSOT + task-level checklist agent + drift alerts + retrospective

title: PO-to-GRN-to-invoice 3-way match
pain_description: PO raised, goods received, invoice issued. matching manual; discrepancies get paid anyway.
santifer_pattern: Doc ingest + 3-way match agent + exception ticketing + GL push

title: Vendor onboarding: tax, banking, W-9/W-8
pain_description: Vendor setup: W-9/W-8, banking, insurance, sanctions. email chain; weeks of back-and-forth.
santifer_pattern: Vendor portal + auto-doc collect + sanctions screen + banking verify + agent chaser

title: T&E policy compliance detection
pain_description: Employees violate T&E policy (meal caps, class of travel); audits random; abuse accumulates.
incumbent_gap: Rule-based; LLM reasoning better at duplicates/manipulations
santifer_pattern: Expense feed + policy RAG + LLM anomaly detection + audit memo

title: Cross-border payment status + refund tracking
pain_description: Customer: 'Where's my payment?'. Team digs across SWIFT, ACH, correspondent banks, local rails. days.
santifer_pattern: Rails connectors + status lookup agent + customer comms + refund orchestrator

title: NetSuite / ERP customization backlog
pain_description: Business asks for custom report / workflow; admin queue 3+ months; consultants charge $200+/hr.
santifer_pattern: Natural-language request → SuiteScript/SuiteFlow generator + sandbox test + PR

title: Salesforce admin backlog (flows, reports, automations)
pain_description: 'Can you add a field and report to this object?'. admin booked weeks out; reps frustrated.
santifer_pattern: NL → SFDC metadata + preview + deploy + test

title: Airtable/Notion maintainer: team base breaks constantly
pain_description: Non-technical ops owns base; team asks for changes; maintainer burned out; data integrity slips.
incumbent_gap: LLMs in UI; not structural change agents
santifer_pattern: NL → schema/formula/view generator + validation + rollback

title: Inbox-zero for small-business owners (non-tech)
pain_description: Inbox drowns small biz; key leads/customers miss; Superhuman too tech; default Gmail too dumb.
incumbent_gap: Either too bare or too premium
santifer_pattern: Low-tech UI + agent triage by topic + auto-drafts + WhatsApp nudge for urgent

title: Revenue leakage: silent pricing/discount drift
pain_description: Renewals happen at old pricing; discounts carry forward; auto-renew terms unfavorable; leakage 3-8% of ARR.
santifer_pattern: Contract ingest + price-list DB + leak detector + renewal recommender
